---
title: "Review — Platelet Ca²⁺ pathway diagram"
subtitle: "Annotation list for the BioRender figure used on slide 7 of `lab-meeting-2026-05-14.pptx`"
author: "Steve Haigh"
date: "2026-05-19"
format:
  pdf:
    pdf-engine: xelatex
    geometry: "margin=2.5cm"
    fontsize: 11pt
    mainfont: "STIX Two Text"
    monofont: "Menlo"
    colorlinks: true
    linkcolor: blue
    urlcolor: blue
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    include-in-header: ../pandoc-header.tex
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
---

# What this is

A review of the BioRender pathway diagram
(`~/Downloads/Platelet Ca Pathway (1).png`) currently used on slide 7 of
the 21 May 2026 lab-meeting deck. Items are grouped by category and
tagged with priority — pick from the top of the **Prioritised fix list**
at the end if time is short.

The diagram is a *whole-cell* style cartoon (compartments + transporters
+ buffers + scope cuts greyed out), not a pathway cartoon. That framing
is the right call and should be preserved.

---

# What's working — preserve

- **Two-arm GPCR cascade is clear.** ADP → P2Y₁ and thrombin →
  PAR1/PAR4 both feed Gα → PLC → IP₃ → IP3R on the DTS. A reader can
  trace the signal at a glance.
- **DTS / SOCE machinery present and correctly placed.** STIM1 on the
  DTS membrane with a dashed link to PM Orai1 captures the puncta
  translocation idea without over-cluttering.
- **Ionotropic vs metabotropic Ca²⁺ entry distinguished.** P2X₁ is
  shown separately with ATP as its ligand (not ADP) — biologically
  correct and a useful teaching point. Keep.
- **Mitochondrial Ca²⁺ handling shown.** MCU (uptake) and NCLX
  (extrusion) both labelled.
- **Buffer pools called out in both compartments.** DTS lumen (CALR,
  HSP90B1, BiP, CREC) and cytosol (CaM + others). This is what
  distinguishes a whole-cell figure from a typical pathway cartoon —
  keep.
- **Scope cuts honestly signalled.** P2Y₁₂, TXA₂R, PKC and granules
  greyed with "not yet in model" — sets audience expectations cleanly.
- **General layout reads naturally.** DTS centre-left, PM machinery
  around the rim, mitochondrion upper-right.

---

# Issues — biology and labelling

## must-fix

1. **`Gα` is under-specified → label `Gαq`.**
   You have two G-protein arms in scope: Gαq (P2Y₁, PAR1/4) and Gαᵢ
   (P2Y₁₂, greyed). Naming the active arm explicitly makes the
   Gᵢ / clopidogrel hook on slide 12 land harder, and pre-empts the
   "which G protein?" question from the audience.

2. **`PLC` → `PLCβ`.**
   PLCβ is the GPCR-coupled isoform; PLCγ is the RTK-coupled story and
   isn't what's running here. One letter, more correct.

3. **`BIP` → `BiP`.**
   Binding immunoglobulin protein; lower-case `i` is the field
   convention. Small but a knowledgeable reader will notice.

## should-fix

4. **Add PIP₂ as the substrate at the PM.**
   IP₃ and DAG currently appear without their lipid source. The model
   includes the PI cycle (`CLAUDE.md`, `K_PI_CYCLE` in
   `calcium_signalling.py`), so omitting PIP₂ understates what's in the
   model. A single labelled PIP₂ blob at the PM is sufficient.

5. **Check every transporter arrow direction.**
   At print resolution it's hard to verify; please confirm:

   | Transporter | Direction       |
   |-------------|------------------|
   | SERCA       | cytosol → DTS    |
   | PMCA        | cytosol → outside |
   | NCX         | cytosol → outside |
   | Orai1       | outside → cytosol |
   | P2X₁        | outside → cytosol |
   | IP3R        | DTS → cytosol     |
   | MCU         | cytosol → mito    |
   | NCLX        | mito → cytosol    |

   A reader unfamiliar with the players will infer the wrong story if
   any arrow reads backwards.

## nice-to-have

6. **Split PAR1 and PAR4 into adjacent receptor boxes.**
   They're distinct proteins with different thrombin affinities (PAR1
   high, PAR4 low — both physiologically active). Splitting them
   matches the proteomic reality cited from Burkhart 2012. Optional —
   depends on space.

7. **Draw a faint ADP arrow to greyed P2Y₁₂ as well.**
   ADP physiologically activates both P2Y₁ and P2Y₁₂; a faint arrow
   into the greyed receptor makes the "Gᵢ arm not yet in model"
   self-evident rather than requiring narration.

8. **Confirm NCLX icon sits on the mitochondrial outline.**
   In the current thumbnail it reads as floating beside it.

---

# Issues — annotations and layout

## should-fix

9. **"PKC not yet in model" label has no connector to DAG.**
   Either add a thin dashed line, or move the label to touch the DAG
   glyph so the association is unambiguous.

10. **"Granules not yet in model" floats in cytosol with no granule
    outline.**
    Consider drawing two faint dashed circles (dense, α) so the
    *absence* is visualised — currently the reader has to take the text
    on trust.

## nice-to-have

11. **Ca²⁺ ion dots are abundant — likely too many.**
    They obscure some receptor labels in the cytosol. Reducing by ~30 %
    would improve readability without losing the visual cue.

12. **Add a small legend.**
    "Grey = not yet in model", "● = Ca²⁺ ion", "→ = directed transport".
    Lets the figure stand alone on a printed handout.

13. **SCS labelled only on the lower-left invagination.**
    The whole open canalicular / surface-connected system is a tubular
    network, not a single bay. Single-bay rendering is fine for a
    schematic; mention verbally so the audience doesn't underestimate
    its surface contribution.

---

# Things I'd not change

- The DTS-lumen and cytosolic **binding-protein callouts**. They are
  what make this a whole-cell rather than a pathway diagram.
- The **greyed-out "not yet in model"** items. Better than omitting;
  signals scope honestly.
- The **general layout**. DTS centre-left, PM machinery around the
  rim, mitochondrion upper-right reads naturally.

---

# Prioritised fix list

Tackle in this order; first three are one-letter or two-letter edits.

| # | Priority      | Change                                                   |
|---|---------------|----------------------------------------------------------|
| 1 | must-fix      | `Gα` → `Gαq`                                             |
| 2 | must-fix      | `PLC` → `PLCβ`                                           |
| 3 | must-fix      | `BIP` → `BiP`                                            |
| 4 | should-fix    | Add PIP₂ glyph at the PM as PLCβ substrate               |
| 5 | should-fix    | Verify every transporter arrow direction (table above)   |
| 6 | should-fix    | Connect "PKC not yet in model" label to DAG              |
| 7 | should-fix    | Draw faint dashed circles for "granules not yet in model"|
| 8 | nice-to-have  | Split PAR1 / PAR4 into adjacent receptors                |
| 9 | nice-to-have  | Faint ADP arrow into greyed P2Y₁₂                        |
| 10| nice-to-have  | Confirm NCLX sits on mitochondrial outline               |
| 11| nice-to-have  | Reduce Ca²⁺ ion dot density ~30 %                        |
| 12| nice-to-have  | Add legend (grey = not in model; ● = Ca²⁺; → = transport)|
